examples.q: A machine does 500 J of work in 10 seconds. What is its power output?
examples.solution:
Power is the RATE at which work is done —
how much energy is transferred per second.

Formula:
    P = W / t

where  P = power   (W — Watts)
       W = work    (J — Joules)
       t = time    (s — seconds)

Step 1 — Identify the values:
    W = 500 J
    t  = 10 s

Step 2 — Substitute and solve:
    P = 500 / 10
    P = 50 W

Answer:  P = 50 W (50 Watts)

Note: 1 Watt = 1 Joule per second (1 W = 1 J/s).

examples.q: A 60 kg person climbs a 4 m staircase in 8 seconds. What is their power output? (g = 10 m/s²)
examples.solution:
First, find the work done against gravity.
Then, divide by time to get power.

Step 1 — Calculate work done (= GPE gained):
    W = m × g × h
    W = 60 × 10 × 4
    W = 2 400 J

Step 2 — Calculate power:
    P = W / t
    P = 2 400 / 8
    P = 300 W

Answer:  P = 300 W

Note: The person's power is the rate at which
they convert chemical energy into GPE.

examples.q: A motor exerts a constant force of 400 N on a vehicle moving at 5 m/s. What is the motor's power output?
examples.solution:
When a constant force acts on an object moving
at constant velocity, use the alternative formula:

    P = F × v

where  F = force    (N)
       v = velocity  (m/s)
       P = power     (W)

This formula comes from:
    P = W/t = (F × d)/t = F × (d/t) = F × v

Step 1 — Identify the values:
    F = 400 N
    v = 5 m/s

Step 2 — Substitute and solve:
    P = 400 × 5
    P = 2 000 W

Answer:  P = 2 000 W  (or 2 kW)

questions.q: A lamp uses 1 200 J of energy in 60 seconds. What is its power rating?
What is the unit of power?
questions.given:
Power = Work / Time = Joules / seconds.
This unit is named after a famous engineer.
questions.sol: The Watt (W) is the unit of power. 1 W = 1 J/s.

questions.q: A 40 W motor runs for 30 seconds. How much work does it do?
questions.given:
Rearrange:  P = W / t
→  W = P × t

questions.q: A person does 900 J of work. If their power output is 150 W, how long did it take?
questions.given:
Rearrange:  P = W / t
→  t = W / P

questions.q: A car engine exerts 3 000 N of force while moving at 20 m/s. What is its power output?
Two machines each do 1 000 J of work. Machine A takes 5 s; Machine B takes 20 s. Which is more powerful?
questions.given:
Formula:  P = W / t
Calculate and compare the power of each machine.
opts: Machine A — P = 200 W vs Machine B — P = 50 W
questions.sol: P_A = 1000/5 = 200 W. P_B = 1000/20 = 50 W. Machine A is 4× more powerful.

questions.q: A 500 W motor lifts a 10 kg load. How fast does it lift the load? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.given:
Force needed to lift = m × g.
Then use:  P = F × v
→  v = P / F

questions.q: How many watts are in 3 kilowatts?
A person runs up stairs, gaining 2 000 J of GPE in 5 seconds. What is their power output?
questions.given:
Work done against gravity = GPE gained.
Formula:  P = W / t

questions.q: A 100 W bulb and a 60 W bulb are both left on for 1 hour. Which uses more energy?
questions.given:
Rearrange:  P = W / t
→  W = P × t
Compare energy used by each bulb.
opts: 100 W bulb — it uses 360 000 J vs 216 000 J
60 W bulb — lower wattage means less power draw
questions.sol: W₁ = 100 × 3600 = 360 000 J. W₂ = 60 × 3600 = 216 000 J. The 100 W bulb uses more energy.
